title: [podman 3.0.1] unqualified image name no longer resolves correctly
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

It's seems to me there is a regression in the way podman resolves unqualified image name.

In version 2.2.1, when I try to pull an image with unqualified name (NOTE: my image is hosted on docker.io), for example:

```
podman pull myorg/reponame
```

podman would go through the list of `unqualified-search-registries` (which is defined in `/etc/containers/registries.conf`), for my case, it is the stock Fedora 33 config:

```
unqualified-search-registries = ["registry.fedoraproject.org", "registry.access.redhat.com", "registry.centos.org", "docker.io"]
```

and it would eventually try each of them until it gets to `docker.io`

When I upgrade to 3.0.1 (part of the latest upgrade of Fedora 33), the same command now prompt me for selection:

```
podman pull myorg/reponame
 - registry.fedoraproject.org/myorg/reponame
 - registry.access.redhat.com/myorg/reponame
 - registry.centos.org/myorg/reponame
 - docker.io/myorg/reponame
```

This is to me considered a breaking changes in behaviour as it causes issues with my automated CI/CD pipeline

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Sat Feb 20 03:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Yes, this is a breaking change - hence the version bump from Podman 2.x to Podman 3.0.

This should only occur when Podman detects it's attached to a TTY, though, so scripts should be unaffected. What CI executor are you using that is showing this?

> This should only occur when Podman detects it's attached to a TTY, though, so scripts should be unaffected. What CI executor are you using that is showing this?

I am using [Buildkite CI/CD](https://buildkite.com) with https://github.com/KennaSecurity/podman-buildkite-plugin plugin

From my understanding the way buildkite works is through the job scheduling with buildkite-agent. So every worker agent must run the buildkite daemon, the documentation does have the `--pty` option (please see https://buildkite.com/docs/agent/v3/cli-bootstrap) but it is not so clear to me if it is enabled or not, I believe it is
@mheon the question is how would I be able to replicate the old behaviour given that podman detects it is attached to a TTY?
